query: What demand charges apply to commercial customers with demand >500 kW in Chicago, based on the OpenEI tariffs data? Answer should contain service class name and demand charge for at least 2 classes
final_answer:
# Commercial Demand Charges in Chicago (ComEd) for Customers with Demand >500 kW

Based on the **OpenEI IURDB** tariff data for **Commonwealth Edison Co (ComEd)** — the utility serving Chicago, IL — the following demand charges apply to commercial customers. The most current tariffs (effective January 1, 2025) are summarized below.

---

## Key Service Classes & Demand Charges

ComEd offers two service types: **RDS** (Retail Delivery Service — delivery only, customer sources own supply) and **BES** (Basic Electric Service — delivery + ComEd's Standard Offer supply). The demand charges are the same across both service types for a given load class and voltage level.

### 1. Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary Voltage)
| Attribute | Value |
|---|---|
| **Service Class Name** | RDS-Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary) / BES-Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary) |
| **Demand Charge** | **$14.58/kW/month** |
| **Voltage** | Secondary |
| **Fixed Charge** | $183.89/month |
| **Source** | [OpenEI IURDB Record](https://apps.openei.org/IURDB/rate/view/67a1068a1f657b662505b680) |

### 2. Very Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary Voltage)
| Attribute | Value |
|---|---|
| **Service Class Name** | RDS-Very Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary) / BES-Very Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary) |
| **Demand Charge** | **$13.10/kW/month** |
| **Voltage** | Secondary |
| **Minimum Demand** | 1,000 kW (`peakkwcapacitymin`) |
| **Fixed Charge** | $154.13/month |
| **Source** | [OpenEI IURDB Record](https://apps.openei.org/IURDB/rate/view/67a38c76820ecb5224036aea) |

### 3. Medium Load Delivery Class (Primary Voltage)
| Attribute | Value |
|---|---|
| **Service Class Name** | RDS-Medium Load Delivery Class (Primary) / BES-Medium Load Delivery Class (Primary) |
| **Demand Charge** | **$8.90/kW/month** |
| **Voltage** | Primary (3-Phase) |
| **Maximum Demand** | 10,000 kW (`peakkwcapacitymax`) |
| **Fixed Charge** | $47.83/month |
| **Source** | [OpenEI IURDB Record](https://apps.openei.org/IURDB/rate/view/67a250b9809958d49f095b5b) |

### 4. Medium Load Delivery Class (Secondary Voltage)
| Attribute | Value |
|---|---|
| **Service Class Name** | RDS-Medium Load Delivery Class (Secondary) / BES-Medium Load Delivery Class (Secondary) |
| **Demand Charge** | **$14.94/kW/month** |
| **Voltage** | Secondary (3-Phase) |
| **Maximum Demand** | 10,000 kW (`peakkwcapacitymax`) |
| **Fixed Charge** | $47.83/month |
| **Source** | [OpenEI IURDB Record](https://apps.openei.org/IURDB/rate/view/67a250da809958d49f095b5d) |

---

## Summary Table

| Service Class | Voltage | Demand Charge ($/kW/mo) | Fixed Charge ($/mo) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Very Large Load** (≥1,000 kW) | Secondary | **$13.10** | $154.13 |
| **Large Load** | Secondary | **$14.58** | $183.89 |
| **Medium Load** | Primary | **$8.90** | $47.83 |
| **Medium Load** | Secondary | **$14.94** | $47.83 |

## Key Takeaways

- **Primary voltage service is significantly cheaper** for demand charges ($8.90/kW vs. $14.58–$14.94/kW at secondary), which incentivizes larger customers (>500 kW) to take service at primary voltage if feasible.
- The **Very Large Load** class (≥1,000 kW) offers a modest discount ($13.10/kW) compared to the Large Load class ($14.58/kW) at secondary voltage.
- These demand charges are **flat (non-time-differentiated)** — they apply uniformly across all 12 months (`flatdemandmonths` = all zeros), meaning there is no seasonal or time-of-use variation in the demand component.
- In addition to the demand charge, customers also pay **energy charges** (per kWh) and various **adjustments** (Carbon-Free Resource Adjustment, Environmental Cost Recovery, Energy Efficiency, etc.).

**Data Source:** OpenEI Utility Rate Database (IURDB), Commonwealth Edison Co (EIA ID: 4110), Commercial sector, Chicago, IL. Tariffs effective January 2025. Original source documents from [ComEd's Current Rates & Tariffs page](https://www.comed.com/MyAccount/MyBillUsage/Pages/CurrentRatesTariffs.aspx).

ComEd offers two service types: **RDS** (Retail Delivery Service — delivery only, customer sources own supply) and **BES** (Basic Electric Service — delivery + ComEd's Standard Offer supply). The demand charges are the same across both service types for a given load class and voltage level.

### 1. Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary Voltage)

| **Service Class Name** | RDS-Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary) / BES-Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary) |

### 2. Very Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary Voltage)

Let me analyze the results to identify the tariffs that apply to commercial customers with demand >500 kW in Chicago. The utility serving Chicago is Commonwealth Edison Co (ComEd).

Let me look at the tariffs and identify which ones are relevant for customers with demand >500 kW. I need to look at the `peakkwcapacitymin` and `peakkwcapacitymax` fields to understand the demand thresholds.

Looking at the most recent tariffs (those with startdate 1735714800, which is January 1, 2025):

1. **RDS-Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** - flatdemandstructure rate: $14.58/kW, no explicit min/max stated in the record
2. **BES-Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** - flatdemandstructure rate: $14.58/kW
3. **RDS-Medium Load Delivery Class (Primary)** - flatdemandstructure rate: $8.90/kW, peakkwcapacitymax: 10,000 kW
4. **BES-Medium Load Delivery Class (Primary)** - flatdemandstructure rate: $8.90/kW, peakkwcapacitymax: 10,000 kW
5. **RDS-Medium Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** - flatdemandstructure rate: $14.94/kW, peakkwcapacitymax: 10,000 kW
6. **BES-Medium Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** - flatdemandstructure rate: $14.94/kW, peakkwcapacitymax: 10,000 kW
7. **RDS-Small Load Delivery Class (Primary)** - flatdemandstructure rate: $6.89/kW, peakkwcapacitymax: 10,000 kW
8. **BES-Small Load Delivery Class (Primary)** - flatdemandstructure rate: $6.89/kW, peakkwcapacitymax: 10,000 kW
9. **RDS-Small Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** - flatdemandstructure rate: $14.57/kW, peakkwcapacitymax: 10,000 kW
10. **BES-Small Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** - flatdemandstructure rate: $14.57/kW, peakkwcapacitymax: 10,000 kW
11. **RDS - Watt-Hour Delivery Class** - no demand charge (no flatdemandstructure)
12. **BES - Watt-Hour Delivery Class** - no demand charge
13. **RDS-Very Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** - flatdemandstructure rate: $13.10/kW, peakkwcapacitymin: 1,000 kW
14. **BES-Very Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** - flatdemandstructure rate: $13.10/kW, peakkwcapacitymin: 1,000 kW

For customers with demand >500 kW, the relevant classes would be:
- The "Large Load" classes (which seem to be for large commercial customers)
- The "Very Large Load" classes (peakkwcapacitymin: 1,000 kW)
- The "Medium Load" classes could also apply depending on the exact demand range

Let me think about which classes specifically apply to >500 kW customers. The ComEd rate structure categorizes customers by load size:
- Small Load: typically smaller commercial
- Medium Load: mid-range commercial
- Large Load: larger commercial
- Very Large Load: peakkwcapacitymin of 1,000 kW

For a customer with demand >500 kW, they would likely fall into the "Large Load" or "Very Large Load" categories, or possibly "Medium Load" depending on ComEd's specific definitions.

Let me focus on the most recent tariffs (2025) and present the key service classes with their demand charges. I'll focus on the ones most clearly applicable to >500 kW customers.

The key demand charges for the most recent (2025) tariffs:

**RDS (Retail Delivery Service) - Delivery only:**
- RDS-Very Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary): $13.10/kW (min 1,000 kW)
- RDS-Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary): $14.58/kW
- RDS-Medium Load Delivery Class (Primary): $8.90/kW
- RDS-Medium Load Delivery Class (Secondary): $14.94/kW

**BES (Basic Electric Service) - Delivery with Standard Offer:**
- BES-Very Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary): $13.10/kW (min 1,000 kW)
- BES-Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary): $14.58/kW
- BES-Medium Load Delivery Class (Primary): $8.90/kW
- BES-Medium Load Delivery Class (Secondary): $14.94/kW
